name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical development, method qualification, and materials characterization across process-development and advanced-material programs. Strong background in HPLC/LC-MS-adjacent workflows, spectroscopy, thermal analysis, DOE/SPC, and technical documentation for cross-functional development teams.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading process development for bio-based polymer systems, applying DOE-driven studies to define critical parameters, improve reproducibility, and establish scale-up-ready process specifications.
Developing analytical workflows to characterize polymer performance, stability-sensitive behavior, and structure-property relationships that guide downstream development decisions.

Developed and executed multi-technique analytical characterization strategies for 15+ organic and organometallic systems using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, NMR, TGA, and DSC to identify degradation pathways and performance-limiting mechanisms.
Drove root-cause analysis and stability-oriented stress testing, generating high-quality datasets and technical conclusions to support iterative materials and process decisions.
Co-led AFOSR-funded thin-film process development, building reproducible deposition and characterization workflows for high-performance molecular coatings in a multidisciplinary research setting.
Developed CVD-based polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring, improving process control and documentation for reproducible film growth.

Managed laboratory operations for a 15-member research group, implemented calibration and tracking systems that improved instrument uptime by 30%, and wrote SOP-style documentation to support safe, reliable experimental execution.

leadership.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
bullets: Mentored 120+ undergraduates and developed supplemental materials that improved exam performance by 15%, sharpening technical communication and training skills useful for method transfer and team support.

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Process
projects.description: Running DOE-guided process optimization and characterization studies to define reproducible operating windows and quality-relevant specifications for polymer systems moving toward scale-up.
projects.tech: DOE, process development, characterization, technical documentation

projects.title: Electrochromic Material Reliability Study
projects.description: Built stability and failure-analysis workflows for electrochromic materials, using multi-technique characterization to connect degradation behavior with design and processing choices.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, TGA, DSC, root-cause analysis

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Supported thin-film process development and reproducible characterization for defense-oriented coatings programs in a cross-functional environment with demanding technical timelines.
projects.tech: Thin films, process development, characterization, cross-functional execution

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.QC / Development Support: Method development, stability-oriented testing, technical documentation, SOP-minded lab operations, data interpretation, specification-minded process support
skills.Statistical Analysis: DOE, Statistical Process Control (SPC, Coursera 2026), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), data visualization
skills.Materials Expertise: Polymers, thin films, organic and organometallic materials, structure-property-processing relationships, degradation analysis
skills.Project Management: Cross-functional execution, multi-project prioritization, stakeholder communication, laboratory coordination
